us-politics.headline: US-Iran Conflict Escalates as Trump Threatens Ground Invasion
us-politics.body:
Trump refused to rule out ground troops if Iran does not reach a nuclear deal, posting an Easter message threatening Iran with 'Hell' over the Strait of Hormuz. Iran claims it destroyed a US aircraft during a recovery mission for a missing service member, while Fox News reports the CIA assisted a separate rescue of an airman behind enemy lines using a deception campaign. The incidents mark a sharp escalation in direct military confrontation.

Opinion in Congress is divided but cautious. House Republicans say ground troops should only be used to seize enriched uranium, not to open the strait by force. Democrats, including Rep. Adam Smith and Rep. Jake Auchincloss, warn that a full war would be costly and that Iran's grip on the strait poses a greater strategic threat than its nuclear program. Former Trump adviser Brett McGurk said the threatening rhetoric makes a deal less likely, not more.

sources.title: Iran says US aircraft destroyed during recovery mission for missing service member
Rep. Madeleine Dean discusses Iran and President Trump's proposed budget
House Republican says he doesn’t think ground troops will be needed to open the Strait of Hormuz
Marjorie Taylor Greene rails against ‘insane’ Trump after Easter Iran threat
Top Armed Services Democrat says Iran war ‘mistake in large part because of the cost’

Trump threatens Iran with ‘Hell’ over Strait of Hormuz in profane post
House Republican: ‘Only purpose’ for US boots on the ground in Iran is ‘to get the enriched uranium’
Former Trump adviser on president’s latest Iran threat: ‘Not going to get a deal’
House Democrat: Iran’s control of Strait of Hormuz ‘more strategically vital’ than nuclear weapon
Inside the daring rescue of airman behind enemy lines: How CIA assisted with 'deception campaign'

us-politics.headline: Cabinet Shake-Up Continues as DHS Funding Lapses
us-politics.body:
Trump fired Attorney General Pam Bondi and Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem within days of each other, then told reporters not to expect further Cabinet changes. SNL mocked Bondi's departure in its cold open. Senator Tim Kaine called the firing a warning to whoever takes the job next, while Trump said he remains satisfied with his broader team.

The firings landed as DHS faces a funding lapse caused by Republican divisions in the House. Speaker Johnson and Senate Majority Leader Thune have struggled to align their caucuses, and Trump has shown little public engagement with the impasse. House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries called on Johnson to cancel recess and return members to Washington. The shutdown leaves DHS operations in limbo at a moment when the administration is simultaneously managing an active military conflict.

sources.title: SNL rips Bondi’s departure in cold open
Republican divisions, Trump’s detachment stymie GOP efforts to reopen DHS
Trump says to not expect Cabinet shake-up after Bondi, Noem firings
Jeffries calls on Johnson to end House recess amid DHS funding lapse

Senate Democrat says Trump ‘needs a lot of prayer’

us-politics.headline: Trump Budget Draws Fire on NASA, Defense and Domestic Spending
us-politics.body:
Trump's proposed budget is drawing scrutiny across multiple fronts. NASA's administrator defended deep proposed cuts, arguing the agency must prioritize lunar and Mars missions over legacy programs. Democrats, including Rep. Madeleine Dean, pushed back on the overall budget framework, citing cuts to domestic programs alongside rising military spending tied to the Iran conflict.

Maryland Governor Wes Moore argued that ending foreign wars would do more to lower gas prices than domestic energy policy changes. An opinion piece in The Hill warned that Democrats risk losing credibility on affordability if they fail to offer concrete economic alternatives ahead of the midterms. The budget debate is shaping up as a central fault line heading into the 2026 election cycle.

sources.title: Rep. Madeleine Dean discusses Iran and President Trump's proposed budget
Moore: Ending foreign wars ‘best thing that we could do’ to lower gas prices
NASA administrator defends Trump’s proposed budget cuts
‘Affordability’ could eventually bite Democrats in the bum
The war in Iran is showing why the US needs diverse energy resources

us-politics.headline: Democrats Eye 2028 as Party Debates Strategy and Outreach
us-politics.body:
Arizona Senator Ruben Gallego said he is seriously considering a 2028 presidential run, telling reporters 'we have to look at it.' His potential candidacy reflects broader Democratic uncertainty about who leads the party after its 2024 losses. No formal moves have been announced.

Rep. Ro Khanna sparked internal debate by defending his appearance with streamer Hasan Piker, arguing that Democrats who refuse to engage with non-traditional media audiences will pay for it at the ballot box. The exchange highlights a live tension inside the party between outreach to younger, online audiences and concerns about association with controversial figures. Both debates point to a party still working out its post-Biden identity.

sources.title: Khanna: Those saying ‘don’t engage’ with personalities like Hasan Piker ‘will cost us future elections’
Gallego on possible 2028 presidential run: ‘We have to look at it’

au-politics.headline: Deeming Secures Top Victorian Liberal Ticket Spot
au-politics.body:
Moira Deeming has won preselection for the top position on the Victorian Liberal Senate ticket, a result that cements her position in the party despite a prolonged internal battle. The outcome is a blow to Liberal moderates who sought to limit her influence following her attendance at a controversial anti-trans rally in 2023.

Deeming's supporters argue the result reflects grassroots member sentiment and a rejection of factional interference from party leadership. Critics warn her prominence will complicate the Liberals' efforts to broaden their appeal in Victoria ahead of the next federal election. The preselection fight has exposed deep divisions within the state party that leadership has so far failed to resolve.

sources.title: Moira Deeming wins Victorian Liberal preselection battle
Australia news live: Moira Deeming secures top spot on Victorian Liberal ticket; Chris Bowen calls for Hormuz reopening

au-politics.headline: Fuel Crisis Reshapes Australian Transport and Politics
au-politics.body:
Soaring fuel prices are driving measurable shifts in Australian behaviour, with e-bike sales surging and used electric vehicle prices rising sharply as drivers seek alternatives. Petrol station owners are also feeling the squeeze, with some questioning whether their businesses remain viable under current conditions.

Energy Minister Chris Bowen has called for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, where Iran has been charging vessels up to US$2 million for passage - a move analysts say signals a new era of maritime economic pressure. The crisis has added urgency to the federal election debate over energy policy, with the government under pressure to demonstrate a credible response to both short-term price pain and longer-term fuel security.

sources.title: Australia news live: Moira Deeming secures top spot on Victorian Liberal ticket; Chris Bowen calls for Hormuz reopening
Commuters are switching from cars to e-bikes to save money on fuel
Price crunch has some servo owners asking: 'Is it really worth doing this?'
One ship paid US$2m to pass Iran. Now we're looking at a new era
‘Doesn’t make sense to hold onto a combustion engine’: used EV prices rise as Australia’s fuel crisis hits

au-politics.headline: Human Rights Commission Probes NSW Racial Discrimination Claim
au-politics.body:
The Australian Human Rights Commission is investigating an allegation of racial discrimination against the NSW government, adding to a series of legal and political pressures on the state over its treatment of Aboriginal communities. Separately, a family court has ordered the return of an Aboriginal child moved 1,700 kilometres from their remote Northern Territory community, ruling the relocation was not in the child's best interests.

The two cases together highlight ongoing tensions between government agencies and Indigenous families over child welfare and decision-making authority. Advocates say both matters reflect systemic failures in how governments consult and engage with Aboriginal communities.

sources.title: Australian Human Rights Commission investigates alleged racial discrimination by NSW government
Aboriginal child moved 1,700km from remote NT community should be returned, family court rules

au-politics.headline: Middle East Escalation Draws Australian Diplomatic Attention
au-politics.body:
Donald Trump has threatened to bomb Iranian power plants in an expletive-laden social media post, raising alarm among analysts who say his shifting statements make any coherent US strategy hard to read. US intelligence has also alleged that Chinese satellite imagery of military bases is being shared with Iran, deepening concerns about the conflict's geopolitical dimensions.

Israel has struck Christian villages in southern Lebanon with no Hezbollah affiliation, drawing international criticism. For Australia, the instability carries direct economic consequences through fuel supply chains, and the Albanese government faces pressure to articulate a clear foreign policy position as the election approaches.

sources.title: Trump's latest tirade shows timeframes 'all but meaningless'
Live: Trump threatens to bomb Iran power plants in expletive-laden post
Christian villages in Lebanon with no affiliation to Hezbollah attacked by Israel
US intelligence says Chinese satellite imagery of bases is helping Iran
One ship paid US$2m to pass Iran. Now we're looking at a new era

world.headline: US-Iran War: Airman Rescued, Hormuz Tensions Escalate
world.body:
A US airman stranded in a remote part of Iran has been rescued in an operation Trump is now calling a victory, though analysts warn significant threats to US forces in the region remain. Iran's internet has been shut down for a record-breaking stretch under wartime conditions, with millions cut off from outside communication.

Trump followed the rescue with an expletive-laden social media post threatening Iran over any attempt to block the Strait of Hormuz, a chokepoint for roughly a fifth of global oil supply. OPEC+ responded by agreeing to hike output, while warning recovery from recent attacks on energy infrastructure will be slow. A BBC crew reporting near the strait described conditions on the edge of an active battlefield.

sources.title: 'We are at the edge of a battlefield': BBC reports near Strait of Hormuz
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Trump issues expletive-laden threat  to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
Analysis: Trump declares victory in Iran war after rescue, but threats to US operation still loom
Trump threatens Iran in expletive-filled social media post
Frustration grows as Iran’s wartime internet shutdown breaks grim record
OPEC+ agrees to hike oil output, warns of slow recovery after attacks

world.headline: Israel Strikes Gaza, Lebanon and Iran Simultaneously
world.body:
Israel carried out strikes across Gaza, Lebanon and Iran in the past 24 hours, killing at least 14 people in Lebanon alone. An Iranian missile struck a residential building in Haifa while another hit an industrial zone in southern Israel, marking continued direct exchanges between the two countries.

In Gaza, an Israeli airstrike hit a crowd in Gaza City as Hamas's armed wing flatly rejected disarmament demands, calling them unacceptable. Christians in Gaza marked Easter under bombardment. Tent camps are now battling rodent infestations alongside the ongoing assault. Israel's far-right minister Ben-Gvir publicly outlined plans for a Palestinian death row, drawing fresh condemnation.

sources.title: Has the humanitarian crisis in Gaza been ignored?
Hamas armed wing says disarmament demands not acceptable

world.headline: Gas Pipeline Bomb Plot Uncovered in Central Europe
world.body:
Serbia and Hungary say explosives were found planted on a Russian gas pipeline running through the region, with Hungary alleging the discovery is linked to a deliberate plot timed ahead of its upcoming election. No group has claimed responsibility.

The incident raises alarm across Central Europe, where energy infrastructure remains a strategic vulnerability. Hungary's government has framed the alleged plot in political terms, though independent verification of the claims is limited.

sources.title: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
Serbia, Hungary say explosives found at Russian gas pipeline

world.headline: Nigeria Church Attack, Libya Migrant Disaster, Ukraine-Syria Pact
world.body:
Nigeria's army rescued 31 hostages following an attack on a church, in the latest episode of militant violence targeting civilians in the country's troubled regions. Separately, dozens of migrants are missing after a boat capsized near Libya in the Mediterranean, adding to a grim toll of sea crossings this year.

On the diplomatic front, Ukraine and Syria announced an agreement to share military and security experience, a notable alignment between two countries both navigating active conflict and post-conflict reconstruction. Ukraine's Zelenskyy framed the deal as part of broader efforts to build security partnerships outside traditional Western channels.

sources.title: Nigeria's army rescues 31 hostages after church attack
Ukraine and Syria to cooperate on security, Zelenskyy says
Dozens missing after boat carrying migrants capsizes near Libya
Dozens missing after migrant boat capsizes in Mediterranean

ai.headline: AI Music Tools Face Copyright Reckoning From Artists
ai.body:
Suno is generating music that closely mimics real artists' styles, raising serious copyright questions that existing law is poorly equipped to answer. A musician has now gone public claiming an AI company cloned her work and is filing claims against her rather than the other way around - flipping the usual power dynamic.

The disputes expose a structural problem: AI music platforms trained on copyrighted recordings are commercially live while litigation and regulation lag years behind. Suno in particular has built a large user base before courts have settled whether its outputs infringe at all. Artists are increasingly finding that by the time legal remedies arrive, the damage is done.

sources.title: Musician says AI company is cloning her music, filing claims against her
Suno is a music copyright nightmare

ai.headline: Google's Gemma 4 Arrives on Phones and Local Machines
ai.body:
Google's Gemma 4 model is now running on iPhones via the AI Edge Gallery app and on local hardware through LM Studio's new headless CLI, paired with Claude Code for agentic workflows. The releases mark a meaningful step toward capable open models that don't require cloud infrastructure.

Separately, Qwen-3.6-Plus became the first model to cross one trillion tokens processed in a single day on OpenRouter, signaling that open and open-weight model usage is scaling fast. Meanwhile, Gemini's integration into Google Maps is drawing positive early reactions, with users reporting it handles day-planning tasks better than expected. The week underscores how quickly AI is moving from cloud APIs into everyday devices and apps.

sources.title: Qwen-3.6-Plus is the first model to break 1T tokens processed in a day
Gemma 4 on iPhone
Running Gemma 4 locally with LM Studio's new headless CLI and Claude Code
I let Gemini in Google Maps plan my day and it went surprisingly well

ai.headline: Iran Threatens OpenAI's Abu Dhabi Data Center With Satellite Video
ai.body:
Iran's government posted a video featuring satellite imagery of OpenAI's 1-gigawatt Stargate data center under construction in Abu Dhabi, threatening its "complete and utter annihilation." The move is a direct escalation targeting the physical infrastructure behind the $30 billion project, which is central to OpenAI and SoftBank's Gulf expansion.

The threat highlights a vulnerability that hyperscale AI buildout has largely ignored: massive, fixed, high-value facilities are soft targets in geopolitically unstable regions. SpaceX is already pitching orbital data centers as a future alternative, partly on the logic that space-based infrastructure is harder to strike or sanction. Whether that pitch gains traction may depend on how seriously investors weigh the risks now on display in Abu Dhabi.

sources.title: Iran threatens 'complete and utter annihilation' of OpenAI's $30B Stargate
Can orbital data centers help justify a massive valuation for SpaceX?

ai.headline: Microsoft Calls Copilot Entertainment, Faces GUI Strategy Critique
ai.body:
Microsoft's terms of service now classify Copilot as being "for entertainment purposes only," a legal hedge that sits awkwardly alongside the company's aggressive push to sell it as a productivity tool for enterprise customers. The disclaimer limits liability but also hands critics an obvious line of attack.

The news lands alongside a sharp essay arguing Microsoft has lacked a coherent graphical interface strategy since Charles Petzold's era, cycling through frameworks without ever committing to one. Together the two stories sketch a company that ships fast and litigates carefully but struggles to project a consistent vision of what its software is actually for.

sources.title: Copilot is ‘for entertainment purposes only,’ according to Microsoft’s terms of use
Microsoft hasn't had a coherent GUI strategy since Petzold

btc.headline: North Korea Behind $270M Drift Exploit, Legal Fallout Follows
btc.body:
Drift Protocol has confirmed that a $270 million exploit was the result of a six-month intelligence operation run by North Korean state actors. The attack was not a smash-and-grab but a sustained, methodical infiltration - raising serious questions about how long the threat went undetected.

A crypto attorney now says Drift may face civil negligence liability, suggesting the platform's security posture will face scrutiny in court, not just in the press. The incident underscores a warning from Ledger's CTO that AI tools are actively lowering the barrier for sophisticated attacks across the crypto industry. State-sponsored hacking and AI-assisted exploits are converging into a threat that most protocols are not built to handle.

sources.title: Crypto attorney says Drift incident may qualify as 'civil negligence'
AI is making crypto's security problem even worse, Ledger CTO warns
Drift says $270 million exploit was a six-month North Korean intelligence operation

btc.headline: Bitcoin Holds at $67K as Geopolitical Pressure Mounts
btc.body:
Bitcoin is stuck near $67,000 with traders warning that new price lows are a matter of time. Sentiment has hit its worst levels since the Iran war began, yet the price has not collapsed - a resilience that analysts attribute partly to ETF-driven institutional flows that now front-run Federal Reserve signals rather than react to them.

Polymarket odds of a US invasion of Iran climbed to 63% following a Trump post, briefly drawing fresh attention to the platform before it pulled controversial Iran-related prediction markets after public backlash. The geopolitical noise is heavy, but Bitcoin's correlation with macro risk assets remains the dominant price driver. A Bitcoin Policy Institute executive described the BTC-dollar relationship as symbiotic, arguing the two assets reinforce rather than undermine each other.

sources.title: Polymarket odds of US invading Iran this year reach 63% after Trump's post
Polymarket pulls controversial Iran rescue markets after intense backlash
Bitcoin and the US dollar have a 'symbiotic' relationship: BPI exec
Bitcoin is now front-running the Fed rather than reacting to it. ETFs are the cause
Bitcoin holds steady as sentiment hits worst levels since Iran war began
New Bitcoin price lows 'matter of time' says trader with BTC stuck at $67K

btc.headline: Quantum Computing Poses Real, Long-Term Threat to Bitcoin
btc.body:
A new explainer from CoinDesk lays out why quantum computing is a genuine long-term threat to Bitcoin's cryptographic foundations. Current quantum machines cannot break Bitcoin's encryption, but the trajectory of the technology means the window to develop post-quantum protections is finite and closing.

The concern is not theoretical panic but an engineering deadline. Bitcoin's elliptic curve signatures, which secure wallets and transactions, are vulnerable to a sufficiently powerful quantum computer. The broader crypto industry has been slow to treat this as urgent. Developers and protocol designers face a hard problem: upgrading cryptographic standards across a decentralized network without a central authority to mandate the change.

sources.title: A simple explainer on what quantum computing actually is, and why it is terrifying for bitcoin

btc.headline: Ant Group Launches Crypto Rails for AI Agent Payments
btc.body:
Ant Group's blockchain division has unveiled a platform that lets AI agents transact directly on crypto infrastructure. The move positions the Alibaba affiliate at the intersection of two of the most active areas in tech, autonomous AI systems and programmable money.

The platform allows AI agents to hold, send, and receive digital assets without human intermediaries at each step. That capability matters because AI agents increasingly need to pay for compute, data, and services in real time. Crypto rails offer a programmable, permissionless settlement layer that traditional payment systems cannot easily replicate. It is an early but concrete signal that AI-native financial infrastructure is being built now, not in the abstract future.

sources.title: Ant Group’s blockchain arm unveils platform for AI agents to transact on crypto rails

food.name: Mafaldine al Limone e Bottarga
food.description: Ruffled ribbon pasta tossed in a bright lemon-butter sauce finished with shaved cured mullet roe
food.tip: Grate the bottarga over the pasta off the heat at the very end — heat destroys its delicate briny complexity

exercise.name: The Sunday Tempo Ladder
exercise.equipment: Running shoes, a flat route or treadmill
exercise.description: A structured run that climbs through three effort zones — 10 min easy, 15 min comfortably hard, 10 min hard, 10 min easy cooldown — teaching your body to shift gears rather than plod at one pace

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity Over Time
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same thing — philosophers from Hobbes to Parfit have weaponised it to interrogate whether *you* are the same person you were ten years ago
learn.why: On a quiet Sunday it's a genuinely unsettling rabbit hole: Derek Parfit's 'Reasons and Persons' reframes the question around psychological continuity and has convinced serious thinkers that personal identity is far less solid — and less important — than we assume
